verses.text: 'A soft answer turneth away wrath: but grievous words stir up anger'; the tongue of the wise uses knowledge aright; 'the eyes of the LORD are in every place, beholding the evil and the good.' Gentle speech calms strife. And all we do is seen by the ever-watching God.

'A soft answer turneth away wrath: but grievous words stir up anger'; the tongue of the wise uses knowledge aright; 'the eyes of the LORD are in every place, beholding the evil and the good.' Gentle speech calms strife. And all we do is seen by the ever-watching God.

'A soft answer turneth away wrath: but grievous words stir up anger'; the tongue of the wise uses knowledge aright; 'the eyes of the LORD are in every place, beholding the evil and the good.' Gentle speech calms strife. And all we do is seen by the ever-watching God.

A wholesome tongue is a tree of life, but perverseness breaks the spirit; a fool despises his father's instruction, but the prudent heeds reproof; in the house of the righteous is much treasure. Words give life or wound. The wise welcome correction and are blessed.

A wholesome tongue is a tree of life, but perverseness breaks the spirit; a fool despises his father's instruction, but the prudent heeds reproof; in the house of the righteous is much treasure. Words give life or wound. The wise welcome correction and are blessed.

A wholesome tongue is a tree of life, but perverseness breaks the spirit; a fool despises his father's instruction, but the prudent heeds reproof; in the house of the righteous is much treasure. Words give life or wound. The wise welcome correction and are blessed.

The lips of the wise disperse knowledge, but not so the foolish heart; 'the sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination to the LORD: but the prayer of the upright is his delight'; He loves the one who follows righteousness. God weighs the heart, not the ritual. He delights in the prayer of the upright.

The lips of the wise disperse knowledge, but not so the foolish heart; 'the sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination to the LORD: but the prayer of the upright is his delight'; He loves the one who follows righteousness. God weighs the heart, not the ritual. He delights in the prayer of the upright.

The lips of the wise disperse knowledge, but not so the foolish heart; 'the sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination to the LORD: but the prayer of the upright is his delight'; He loves the one who follows righteousness. God weighs the heart, not the ritual. He delights in the prayer of the upright.

Correction is grievous to him that forsakes the way, and he that hates reproof shall die; 'hell and destruction are before the LORD,' how much more the hearts of men; a scorner loves not his reprover. God sees all hearts. The wise accept rebuke; scorners flee from it to ruin.

Correction is grievous to him that forsakes the way, and he that hates reproof shall die; 'hell and destruction are before the LORD,' how much more the hearts of men; a scorner loves not his reprover. God sees all hearts. The wise accept rebuke; scorners flee from it to ruin.

Correction is grievous to him that forsakes the way, and he that hates reproof shall die; 'hell and destruction are before the LORD,' how much more the hearts of men; a scorner loves not his reprover. God sees all hearts. The wise accept rebuke; scorners flee from it to ruin.

A merry heart makes a cheerful face, but sorrow breaks the spirit; the discerning heart seeks knowledge, the fool feeds on folly; 'he that is of a merry heart hath a continual feast.' Contentment is a feast. A glad, trusting heart turns every day into plenty.

A merry heart makes a cheerful face, but sorrow breaks the spirit; the discerning heart seeks knowledge, the fool feeds on folly; 'he that is of a merry heart hath a continual feast.' Contentment is a feast. A glad, trusting heart turns every day into plenty.

A merry heart makes a cheerful face, but sorrow breaks the spirit; the discerning heart seeks knowledge, the fool feeds on folly; 'he that is of a merry heart hath a continual feast.' Contentment is a feast. A glad, trusting heart turns every day into plenty.

'Better is little with the fear of the LORD than great treasure and trouble therewith'; better a dinner of herbs with love than a fatted ox with hatred; a wrathful man stirs strife, but the slow to anger appeases it. Peace beats plenty. Love and the fear of God outweigh riches and feasting.

'Better is little with the fear of the LORD than great treasure and trouble therewith'; better a dinner of herbs with love than a fatted ox with hatred; a wrathful man stirs strife, but the slow to anger appeases it. Peace beats plenty. Love and the fear of God outweigh riches and feasting.

'Better is little with the fear of the LORD than great treasure and trouble therewith'; better a dinner of herbs with love than a fatted ox with hatred; a wrathful man stirs strife, but the slow to anger appeases it. Peace beats plenty. Love and the fear of God outweigh riches and feasting.

The slothful man's way is a hedge of thorns, but the upright's is plain; a wise son gladdens his father, a foolish despises his mother; folly is joy to the senseless, but the man of understanding walks uprightly. Diligence smooths the path. The wise find joy in walking straight.

The slothful man's way is a hedge of thorns, but the upright's is plain; a wise son gladdens his father, a foolish despises his mother; folly is joy to the senseless, but the man of understanding walks uprightly. Diligence smooths the path. The wise find joy in walking straight.

The slothful man's way is a hedge of thorns, but the upright's is plain; a wise son gladdens his father, a foolish despises his mother; folly is joy to the senseless, but the man of understanding walks uprightly. Diligence smooths the path. The wise find joy in walking straight.

Without counsel purposes fail, but with many advisers they stand; a man has joy by a fitting answer, and 'a word spoken in due season, how good is it!' The way of life is upward for the wise, to depart from hell beneath. Counsel and timely words bless. Wisdom's path leads upward, away from ruin.

Without counsel purposes fail, but with many advisers they stand; a man has joy by a fitting answer, and 'a word spoken in due season, how good is it!' The way of life is upward for the wise, to depart from hell beneath. Counsel and timely words bless. Wisdom's path leads upward, away from ruin.

Without counsel purposes fail, but with many advisers they stand; a man has joy by a fitting answer, and 'a word spoken in due season, how good is it!' The way of life is upward for the wise, to depart from hell beneath. Counsel and timely words bless. Wisdom's path leads upward, away from ruin.

The LORD destroys the proud's house but establishes the widow's border; the thoughts of the wicked are abomination, but pleasant words are pure; 'he that is greedy of gain troubleth his own house,' while he that hates bribes shall live. God defends the lowly. Greed ruins a home; integrity preserves it.

The LORD destroys the proud's house but establishes the widow's border; the thoughts of the wicked are abomination, but pleasant words are pure; 'he that is greedy of gain troubleth his own house,' while he that hates bribes shall live. God defends the lowly. Greed ruins a home; integrity preserves it.

The LORD destroys the proud's house but establishes the widow's border; the thoughts of the wicked are abomination, but pleasant words are pure; 'he that is greedy of gain troubleth his own house,' while he that hates bribes shall live. God defends the lowly. Greed ruins a home; integrity preserves it.

The righteous heart studies how to answer, but the wicked pours out evil; 'the LORD is far from the wicked: but he heareth the prayer of the righteous'; the light of the eyes and a good report cheer the heart. The godly weigh their words. God hears the righteous, and good news refreshes the soul.

The righteous heart studies how to answer, but the wicked pours out evil; 'the LORD is far from the wicked: but he heareth the prayer of the righteous'; the light of the eyes and a good report cheer the heart. The godly weigh their words. God hears the righteous, and good news refreshes the soul.

The righteous heart studies how to answer, but the wicked pours out evil; 'the LORD is far from the wicked: but he heareth the prayer of the righteous'; the light of the eyes and a good report cheer the heart. The godly weigh their words. God hears the righteous, and good news refreshes the soul.

The ear that heeds the reproof of life abides among the wise; he that refuses instruction despises his own soul, but he that hears reproof gets understanding; 'the fear of the LORD is the instruction of wisdom; and before honour is humility.' Teachableness is wisdom. Humility is the road to honour.

Teachableness is wisdom. Humility is the road to honour.
